old_claim.note: Madrigals & Polyphonic Music provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Petrucci's 1501 anthology predates the graph's later madrigal node; a later repertoire category cannot enable the earlier printing process.

source_shape.source_locator: The Library of Congress identifies the 1501 Odhecaton as the first movable-type book of polyphonic music and first printed music anthology.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The bibliographic record anchors Petrucci's printing to 1501, before the graph's later madrigal category, and supports printing rather than that genre as the production dependency.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. Petrucci's 1501 anthology predates the graph's later madrigal node; a later repertoire category cannot enable the earlier printing process.

ontology_before: printed_music_publishing treated madrigals_polyphonic_music as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: Petrucci's 1501 anthology predates the graph's later madrigal node; a later repertoire category cannot enable the earlier printing process.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_music_publishing no longer has madrigals_polyphonic_music as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes madrigals_polyphonic_music as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped printed_music_publishing claim.
